title: UCLA CS 111 (Winter 2017) Operating Systems Labs
summary: A multi-lab OS course assignment in C and Python covering: file I/O with signal handling (Lab 0), a simple shell using fork/dup2/execvp (Lab 1A), multithreaded synchronization with a shared counter and a sorted linked list (Lab 2A), an ext2 filesystem image parser (Lab 3A), and a Python filesystem analyzer with a syntax fix (Lab 3B). All units are independent except that the Lab 2A list harness depends on the SortedList data structure module.
instructionPreview: This is an undergraduate operating-systems lab assignment. The repository contains several labs covering C systems programming: file I/O, a simple shell, multithreaded synchronization, sorted linked lists, an ext2 filesystem parser, and a Python filesystem analyzer.
instruction:
This is an undergraduate operating-systems lab assignment. The repository
contains several labs covering C systems programming: file I/O, a simple shell, multithreaded
synchronization, sorted linked lists, an ext2 filesystem parser, and a Python filesystem
analyzer.

Your working directory is `/workspace`. The project code is already present.

You must complete the following incomplete implementations:

1. **`Lab 0/lab0.c`** — The `main` function's file-copy loop is missing. After parsing
   `--input` and `--output` options (which redirect stdin/stdout to files), read from
   file descriptor 0 one byte at a time using `read()` and write each byte to file
   descriptor 1 using `write()`, stopping at EOF. Exit with `errno` on read error, exit
   with 0 on success.

2. **`Lab 1/Lab 1A/simpsh.c`** — The main function's option-processing while loop and
   file-opening logic are missing. Using the provided `longOptions` struct and `getopt_long`,
   implement:
   - `--rdonly FILE` / `--wronly FILE`: open the specified file with `open(optarg, flags)`,
     track num open files, print verbose output if `--verbose` was set, handle errors by
     setting exitStatus to 1.
   - `--command i o e cmd args`: parse 3 file descriptor indices followed by a command
     and arguments (terminated by `--` or end of argv). Fork a child process, redirect
     fds 0/1/2 via `close()`+`dup2()` (adding 3 to map logical fd to actual fd), and
     `execvp()` the command. Print verbose output if set.
   - Exit with `exitStatus` at the end.

3. **`Lab 2/Lab 2A/SortedList.c`** — Four functions in this doubly-linked sorted list
   module are stubbed out:
   - `SortedList_insert(list, element)`: Insert into sorted position (ascending by key via
     `strcmp`), maintaining circular doubly-linked structure. Yield if `opt_yield & INSERT_YIELD`.
   - `SortedList_delete(element)`: Validate prev/next consistency (return 1 if corrupted),
     unlink and `free` the element, return 0. Yield if `opt_yield & DELETE_YIELD`.
   - `SortedList_lookup(list, key)`: Traverse from `list->next`, return first matching
     element or NULL. Yield if `opt_yield & LOOKUP_YIELD`.
   - `SortedList_length(list)`: Count elements validating consistency (return -1 if
     corrupted). Yield if `opt_yield & LOOKUP_YIELD`.

4. **`Lab 2/Lab 2A/lab2_add.c`** — Five functions are stubbed out:
   - `add(pointer, value)`: Compute `sum = *pointer + value`, call `sched_yield()` if
     `opt_yield` is set, then store `*pointer = sum`.
   - `mutexAdd(pointer, value)`: Same as `add` but wrapped in `pthread_mutex_lock/unlock(&lock)`.
   - `spinLockAdd(pointer, value)`: Same as `add` but wrapped in `lockSpinLock/unlockSpinLock`.
   - `compareAndSwapAdd(pointer, value)`: Use a `do { temp = *pointer; temp2 = temp + value; }
     while(__sync_val_compare_and_swap(pointer, temp, temp2) != temp)` loop, yielding in
     the loop body if `opt_yield`.
   - `threadIterations(threadArgs)`: Cast args to `struct threadIterationsArgs*`, loop
     `numIterations` times calling the appropriate add variant (based on `syncOption`:
     NULL→add, 'm'→mutexAdd, 's'→spinLockAdd, 'c'→compareAndSwapAdd) with value +1,
     then loop again with value -1, then `pthread_exit(NULL)`.

5. **`Lab 2/Lab 2A/lab2_list.c`** — Two functions are stubbed out:
   - `threadIterations(threadArg)`: Cast arg to get `threadID`. Acquire mutex/spinlock if
     `syncOption` is 'm'/'s'. Insert this thread's elements (indices `threadID, threadID+numThreads,
     ...`) into the list via `SortedList_insert`. Get `SortedList_length`. Look up and delete
     each inserted element (exit(1) on failure). Release lock. `pthread_exit(NULL)`.
   - `generateRandomKey()`: Allocate a 10-char C-string, fill the first 9 positions with
     random characters from `"ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuv123456789"`,
     null-terminate, and return the key.

6. **`Lab 3/Lab 3A/lab3a.c`** — Four functions for an ext2 filesystem image parser are
   stubbed out. The `superblockData` struct and `isPowerOfTwo` helper are provided.
   - `superblockAnalysis(fileSystemImage)`: Read the superblock from offset 1024 (32 bytes),
     populate the global `superblockData` struct fields (magicNumber, totalNumInodes,
     totalNumBlocks, blockSize as `1024 << s_log_block_size`, fragmentSize, blocksPerGroup,
     inodesPerGroup, fragmentsPerGroup, firstDataBlock, inodeSize). Write a CSV line to
     `super.csv` with these fields.
   - `groupDescriptorAnalysis(fileSystemImage)`: For each block group, read the 32-byte
     group descriptor. Write group info to `group.csv`. Then scan the block and inode
     bitmaps, writing free block/inode numbers to `bitmap.csv`. For each allocated inode,
     read its 128-byte inode entry and write to `inode.csv`.
   - `processDirectoryEntriesInBlock(fileSystemImage, blockPointer, directorycsv, entryNumber,
     parentInodeNumber)`: Read directory entries from a data block, writing each entry's
     parent inode, entry number, entry length, name length, name, and inode number to
     `directorycsv`.
   - `directoryEntriesAnalysis(fileSystemImage)`: Iterate over all allocated inodes that
     are directories (mode & 0x4000), read their 12 direct block pointers and the indirect
     block pointer from the inode, and call `processDirectoryEntriesInBlock` for each
     non-zero block pointer.

7. **`Lab 3/Lab 3B/lab3b.py`** — Fix the Python syntax error (inconsistent tabs/spaces
   in indentation around line 132) so the file compiles without a `TabError`.

moduleDag.description: A multi-lab OS course assignment in C and Python covering: file I/O with signal handling (Lab 0), a simple shell using fork/dup2/execvp (Lab 1A), multithreaded synchronization with a shared counter and a sorted linked list (Lab 2A), an ext2 filesystem image parser (Lab 3A), and a Python filesystem analyzer with a syntax fix (Lab 3B). All units are independent except that the Lab 2A list harness depends on the SortedList data structure module.

nodes.label: Lab 0: File Copy
nodes.description: Reads bytes one-at-a-time from stdin (fd 0) using read() and writes each to stdout (fd 1) using write(), stopping at EOF and exiting with errno on read error. Also handles --input/--output file redirection and --segfault/--catch signal options.

nodes.label: Lab 1A: Simple Shell
nodes.description: Minimal shell using getopt_long to process --rdonly, --wronly, --command, and --verbose options. File-opening options accumulate open fds; --command forks a child, redirects fds 0/1/2 via dup2 (adding 3 to map logical to actual fd), and execvp's the command.

nodes.label: Lab 2A: SortedList Data Structure
nodes.description: Circular doubly-linked list maintaining ascending key order via strcmp. Implements SortedList_insert, SortedList_delete (with corruption check), SortedList_lookup (linear scan), and SortedList_length (with consistency validation). Each operation optionally yields via sched_yield to expose races.

nodes.label: Lab 2A: Threaded Counter
nodes.description: Multi-threaded counter benchmark implementing four add variants: unprotected add, mutex-protected mutexAdd, spin-lock-protected spinLockAdd, and CAS-based compareAndSwapAdd. Thread worker threadIterations adds +1 then -1 N times using the selected variant and exits with pthread_exit.

nodes.label: Lab 2A: Threaded SortedList Harness
nodes.description: Multi-threaded test harness for the SortedList. Each thread inserts its designated elements, checks the list length, looks up and deletes each element, optionally guarded by mutex or spin-lock. Also implements generateRandomKey() to produce 9-character random alphanumeric keys.

nodes.label: Lab 3A: ext2 Filesystem Parser
nodes.description: Parses an ext2 filesystem image: reads the superblock and writes super.csv; iterates block group descriptors, scans block/inode bitmaps for free entries (bitmap.csv), and reads allocated inodes (inode.csv, group.csv); traverses directory inodes following direct, indirect, doubly- and triply-indirect block pointers to emit directory.csv and indirect.csv.

nodes.label: Lab 3B: Python Filesystem Analyzer
nodes.description: Python script that analyzes filesystem CSV data for consistency; requires fixing a TabError caused by mixed tabs/spaces indentation around line 132 so the file compiles and executes without a syntax error.

edges.label: lab2_list.c includes SortedList.h and calls all four SortedList functions (insert, length, lookup, delete); it cannot compile or pass tests until the SortedList implementation is complete
